name: Roast Quail with Juniper Berries III (Quail)
recipeIngredient: 12 each Quail, necks and feet - removed
6 slices Pancetta, thin (Italian dry-cured unsmoked bacon) coarsely chopped
12 fresh Sage leaves, or ½ tsp dried Sage
36 toasted Juniper Berries - in a dry skillet for 5 minutes
recipeInstructions:
See recipe for Veal Stock, Gorgonzola Polenta, and Cranberry Relish.
Preheat your oven to 450°F. Holding quail, breast up, tuck wing tips under. Place a little pancetta, sage leaf, and 2 juniper berries in the cavity of each bird. Sprinkle the cavity and outside of each bird with salt and pepper. Push each leg joint downward and fasten it to the carcass with a toothpick.
Heat olive oil in a large heavy skillet over high heat. Arrange birds in pan, breasts down. Sauté, shaking pan occasionally until breasts are lightly golden, 2-3 minutes. Place skillet in oven and roast for 5-6 minutes. Use tongs to turn bird's breasts up and continue to roast until golden brown, about 6 minutes. Remove the skillet from the oven and transfer quail to warm serving plates; keep warm while preparing sauce.
Discard any fat from the skillet and place pan over medium-high heat. Deglaze with gin and wine, scraping up any browned bits in a pan. Add stock and boil gently until reduced enough to coat a spoon lightly. Stir in remaining juniper berries, adjust seasonings, and pour over birds. Serve immediately.
